name: Matagalls from the Bordoriol pass - Circular in the Montseny Natural Park
name_original: Matagalls desde el collado de Bordoriol - Circular en el Parque Natural del Montseny
region_intro: Hiking around Viladrau offers access to the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, characterized by mountainous and wooded terrain. The region features diverse forests, including ancient chestnut, beech, and fir trees, alongside nearly 200 springs, rivers, and waterfalls. Matagalls Mountain provides a prominent peak, and the area is bordered by the Guilleries Massif, contributing to its rugged landscape. Best hiking trails around Viladrau The most popular hiking route is PR-C 205 Viladrau to Matagalls Trail, a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route offers extensive panoramic views from the summit of Matagalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Matagalls from the Bordoriol pass - Circular in the Montseny Natural Park, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This circular route explores the varied terrain within the Montseny Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Chestnut of the Nine Branches Trail, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through ancient chestnut trees and springs, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Hiking around Viladrau is defined by the diverse forests of Montseny Natural Park, the iconic Matagalls Mountain, and numerous water sources. The network offers options for different ability levels, from family-friendly walks to demanding mountain expeditions. The routes in Viladrau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Viladrau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Viladrau?
A: Viladrau offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 distinct trails. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain expeditions, catering to all levels of experience within the Montseny Natural Park.

Q: What makes hiking in Viladrau unique?
A: Hiking in Viladrau is unique due to its privileged location within the Montseny Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. The region boasts diverse landscapes, including ancient chestnut, beech, and fir forests, nearly 200 natural springs, and the iconic Matagalls Mountain. This rich biodiversity and varied terrain offer a truly immersive natural experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Viladrau?
A: The trails around Viladrau are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse forests, the panoramic views from peaks like Matagalls, and the abundance of natural water sources. Many appreciate the well-maintained paths and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Viladrau?
A: Yes, Viladrau has several easy and family-friendly routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359220947/chestnut-of-the-nine-branches-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut of the Nine Branches Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a low-difficulty circular route that winds through ancient chestnut trees and springs. Another excellent option is the Ruta de les Fonts (Fountain Route), an easy walk allowing visitors to discover many of the area's numerous springs.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Viladrau?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938594746/pr-c-205-viladrau-to-matagalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-C 205 Viladrau to Matagalls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.5-mile route that offers extensive panoramic views from the summit of Matagalls Mountain. This iconic peak provides rewarding ascents and stunning vistas of the Pyrenees and the Vic plain on clear days.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Viladrau?
A: Yes, Viladrau offers several excellent circular hiking routes. A notable moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944255953/matagalls-from-the-bordoriol-pass-circular-in-the-montseny-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matagalls from the Bordoriol pass - Circular in the Montseny Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which explores varied terrain. For a historical and scenic loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949665218/sant-segimon-and-mare-de-deu-l-erola-from-viladrau-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Segimon and Mare de Déu l'Erola from Viladrau – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or springs?
A: Viladrau is renowned for its abundant water sources, with nearly 200 springs. The popular Ruta de les Fonts (Fountain Route) is an easy walk specifically designed to showcase many of these springs. Additionally, the nearby Gorges area features highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2757495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Sort&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg Negre de Rentadors&lt;/a&gt;, offering opportunities to see waterfalls and gorges.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Montseny Natural Park near Viladrau?
A: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Montseny Natural Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. It's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected areas, to confirm any restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Viladrau?
A: The best time to hike in Viladrau largely depends on your preferences. Spring offers lush greenery and blooming flora, while autumn is spectacular with the changing colors of the chestnut and beech forests. Summer provides warm weather, but higher elevations offer cooler temperatures. Winter hiking is possible, but some higher routes may require appropriate gear due to snow or ice.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Yes, Viladrau's trails often blend natural beauty with historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227965/mas-la-sala-return-from-viladrau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas la Sala&lt;/a&gt; route leads to a fortified farmhouse with historical connections to the bandit Serrallonga. You can also find ancient bridges across streams, some forming part of the 'Els Ponts Perduts' (Lost Bridges) trail, adding historical charm to your walks.

Q: Are there any nearby attractions or points of interest accessible from Viladrau?
A: Beyond the trails, Viladrau is close to several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vallforners Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Fe Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic lakeside views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2679945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Romà de Sau (Submerged Bell Tower)&lt;/a&gt; is a unique sight. For an interpretive experience, visit the Arborètum de Bruixes i Bandolers (Arboretum of Witches and Bandits) or Espai Montseny, the Natural Park's interpretation center.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Viladrau?
A: Yes, many trailheads around Viladrau offer designated parking areas. The village itself has parking facilities, and specific routes often have small parking lots at their starting points. It's advisable to check route descriptions on komoot for details on parking availability for individual trails.
tips:
Example of a Montseny forest spring located in a beech forest. It was prepared for use in 1986.
Its name is due to the fact that very close to this source, they hunted the last female with her cubs that inhabited the Montseny area in the 20th century.
Of the many springs that Montseny and Matagalls have, this is perhaps the most frequented, due to the proximity of the summit and the shade there.

el Matagalls is an iconic hiking mountain in Catalonia, visited for generations.
Its summit can be reached by several routes, all with their own charm. The easy access of del Coll Formic means that there is currently a considerable flood of visitors of all kinds who degrade the environment in a worrying way.
It was the starting point of the legendary resistance march "Matagalls - Montserrat" created in 1972 by the Gràcia Hiking Center but, due to the environmental impact around this peak, since 1998 the start has been made at Coll Formic to move from Brull to 2015.

Monolith dedicated to the cello master and messenger of peace around the world. It was built in 1975.
In it you can read in Catalan:
"Now comes the time of the great rest
and peace in the mountains;
the first cold withered the flowers;
spiders make silver veils.
Now it starts beyond the field
the august tomb of nature;
the beech already has a copper color
and it's the golden chickadee that shines."
-Felip Graugés-
